input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm the name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it (includes android, ios sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows SDK and WinUI/XAML tooling. The simplest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, for a minimal CLI-only install with required components:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add the needed components via vs_installer:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --passive --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload installs a minimal Android SDK, but you may need to manage versions explicitly.

```powershell
# Find where the MAUI workload put the Android SDK
dotnet android sdk info

# Alternatively, install Android Studio CLI tools standalone:
winget install Google.AndroidStudio
# Or just the command-line tools:
# Download from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only

# Discover available platforms & build-tools dynamically
$sdkmanager = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-"
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"

# Install the latest stable API level and build tools
# (replace versions with what --list showed)
& $sdkmanager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses
```

## Step 5 — Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Point to the Android SDK (adjust path if different)
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk",
  "User"
)

# Add platform-tools to PATH for adb
$path = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
$newEntry = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\platform-tools"
if ($path -notlike "*$newEntry*") {
  [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
    "Path", "$path;$newEntry", "User"
  )
}
```

## Step 6 — Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdmanager = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"
$sdkmanager = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# List available system images
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "system-images;android-"

# Install a system image (pick the latest from the list above)
& $sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create the AVD
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Verify
& $avdmanager list avd
```

## Step 7 — Enable Windows Developer Mode

Required for deploying WinUI/MAUI apps locally:

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 8 — Scaffold, Build, and Run

```powershell
# Create a new MAUI project
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Deploy to running emulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## Step 9 — Verify Everything

```powershell
# Quick health check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\platform-tools\adb.exe" devices
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with `net8.0` if you installed .NET 8 instead.
- The `--list` commands in steps 4/6 let you **discover versions dynamically** rather than hardcoding them.
- If you skip Visual Studio entirely, you still need the Windows SDK (`winget install Microsoft.WindowsSDK.10.0.19041`).
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
